name: Sourdough San Francisco Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large warmed bowl, sprinkle the dry yeast over the warm water and stir to dissolve.
2. Let stand until bubbly, about 5-10 minutes.
3. Blend in the sourdough starter, sugar, salt, and cider vinegar.
4. Gradually beat in 3 cups of white flour.
5. Beat for at least 3 minutes.
6. Turn batter into a large oiled glass or ceramic bowl, cover with a towel, and let rise in warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
7. Combine 1 cup of the remaining flour with baking soda.
8. Stir the batter down and add the flour-baking soda mixture.
9. Gradually add the remaining flour to make a stiff dough. Turn out onto a floured board and knead, adding additional flour only as needed to prevent sticking, about 300 strokes of folding until smooth and elastic.
10. Sprinkle a greased baking sheet with cornmeal.
11. Form dough into 2 oblong loaves and place on the sheet.
12. Cover with a towel and let rise in warm place for 1-1/2 hours, or until nearly doubled in size.
13. With a sharp razor, slash the tops of the loaves diagonally.
14. Mist with water and bake in a preheated oven at 450°F for 10 minutes.
15. Reduce heat to 400°F and continue baking 35 minutes longer, or until bread tests done.
16. For a harder crust, place a pan of hot water on the bottom of the oven and mist with water several times during baking.
17. Remove the pan of water after 15 minutes of baking.
18. Turn out onto a wire rack and cool.
